mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding aggregate income and consumption, driving corporate revenue expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not positive in greater than 50% of months with rising PAYEMS over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is pinned below 0.25%, persistent low discount rates and search-for-yield dynamics force equity valuations higher, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices paired with an unchanged federal funds rate create negative real policy rates that stimulate borrowing and risk asset demand, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint z-score interaction term between CPIAUCSL change and FEDFUNDS level shows Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During post-recession recovery, quarterly real GDP growth acceleration reflects demand normalization that lifts cyclical earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns following positive GDPC1 surprises are not significantly positive during recovery periods over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production reflect contracting manufacturing activity and corporate revenue compression, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances following two consecutive monthly declines in INDPRO.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts amplify household sector momentum, generating positive wealth effects and S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days beyond either variable alone.
failure_condition: Linear interaction term between RSAFS and HOUST monthly changes fails to achieve Rank IC greater than 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index acceleration above 2.5% annualized triggers fears of impending monetary tightening, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are nonnegative in more than 50% of months when CPIAUCSL annualized change exceeds 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate decline exceeding 0.2 percentage points reflects rapid labor market tightening that boosts consumer confidence and spending, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of months when UNRATE drops by more than 0.2 percentage points.
